references.title: Evaluating the effectiveness of educational data mining techniques for early prediction of students' academic failure in introductory programming courses

extracted.Data sources: Student Characteristics, LMS logs, Performance throughout the course
extracted.Features: [age, gender, civil status, city, income, student registration, period, class, semester, campus, access frequency, forum participation, received and viewed files, educational tool usage, year of enrollment, status, performance in weekly activities and exams]
extracted.Preprocessing Details:
Authors use Information Gain to perform Feature Selection. 

Class Imbalance was addressed using SMOTE.

Moreoever, authors perform hypar-parameter tuning.
extracted.Comments:
Authors do a work that uses ML to do early prediction of performance in a course.

They compare multiple algorithms and notice that Fine-Tuning and Preprocessing tend to improve model performance.
extracted.Limitations:
• No model assessment strategy

  • Single institution data

  • Small dataset

  • Limited parameter search space

annotations.text: The authors perform a comparative study of different predictive models on 2 distinct datasets obtained from a Brazilian University on a course of introductory programming:
